name: Canada

colors.symbolism: one of Canada's national colours, proclaimed by King George V in 1921; recalls the cross of Saint George and the sacrifice of Canadians in the First World War
Canada's other national colour, evoking the snow and the lily of France

description: A vertical triband in the proportions 1:2:1 — red, white, red — with a stylised red 11-pointed maple leaf centred on the broad white square (the Canadian Pale).
history: Adopted on 15 February 1965 — now observed as National Flag of Canada Day — replacing the Canadian Red Ensign that had served as a de facto national flag since the late 19th century. The design was selected after the long and politically charged 'Great Canadian Flag Debate' under Prime Minister Lester B. Pearson; the chosen single-leaf design is generally credited to George F. G. Stanley, with final artwork by Jacques Saint-Cyr. Proclaimed by Queen Elizabeth II on 28 January 1965 to take effect 15 February 1965.

features.symbolism: the red-white-red Canadian Pale, derived from the design of the Royal Military College of Canada flag; the red bands recall the colours of England and France, the historic colonising powers

a long-standing emblem of Canada, dating in popular use to at least the 1830s; the stylised eleven-point shape was chosen for clarity at a distance and bears no political meaning in the number of points
